objective: Fix the standalone LIVE-NESTED (gpuwrf.cli run --max-dom N) 24 h OOM: 'RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED: Out of memory trying to allocate 9.24 GiB'. Make the nested forecast memory-bounded so it fits the 32 GiB RTX 5090 for a full 24 h.
case_note: the EXACT production failing case (3 domains, fresh real.exe wrfinput_d01/d02/d03 + wrfbdy_d01, NO CPU-WRF wrfout).
device: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090, 32607 MiB total (~3 GiB held by desktop apps krunner/chrome/plasmashell).

root_cause.summary: The 9.24 GiB allocation is a SINGLE recurring XLA temporary: the RRTMG short+longwave g-point radiation intermediate on the d02 grid, allocated every radiation step inside the per-domain advance (_advance_chunk) AND every output interval inside compute_m9_diagnostics (the wrfout writer). It is NOT state/output accumulation: peak in-use is FLAT with lead.
root_cause.why_24h_OOMs: peak in-use (~9 GiB) is far below the ~29 GiB available, so a 24 h OOM at 'allocate 9.24 GiB' is BFC-ARENA FRAGMENTATION: the default XLA BFC allocator (with XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_PREALLOCATE=false) interleaves the big recurring transient with thousands of tiny eager nesting boundary-build buffers over a 4800-root-step run, so it can no longer find a CONTIGUOUS 9.24 GiB block even though enough total memory is free.
root_cause.v0120_regression: the v0.11.0 24 h nested proof used a LIGHTWEIGHT output callback (surface_layer_diagnostics only). The v0.12.0 standalone nested_pipeline writer added the FULL compute_m9_diagnostics (re-runs RRTMG) per output interval -- another 8.21 GiB transient on top of the per-step radiation transient.

evidence_alloc_fragmentation.BFC_default: alloc 6 GiB then free then alloc 1 GiB -> footprint RETAINED (8722 -> 8722 MiB): the arena holds freed memory and fragments.
evidence_alloc_fragmentation.platform_allocator: same sequence -> footprint DROPS (6676 -> 1556 MiB): synchronous cudaMalloc/cudaFree, no arena, cannot fragment.

part1_segmentation.what: Drive the SAME validated run_operational_domain_tree recursion in OUTPUT-INTERVAL segments (one wrfout hour of root steps each) from a host loop, carrying the device carries + the GLOBAL step clock (own_steps) across segments and block_until_ready-ing + dropping the prior segment's result between segments. The nested analogue of run_forecast_operational_segmented; bounds the working set and gives the allocator clean per-hour coalescing windows.
part1_segmentation.domain_tree_change: run_domain_tree_callbacks/run_operational_domain_tree gained optional initial_own_steps + resumable carries (default behaviour unchanged).
part1_segmentation.wrf_faithful: physics/dynamics and the live parent->child boundary coupling are UNTOUCHED. Proven byte-identical step+output schedule to a single full-length call (CPU callback test: identical advance step sequence, identical own_steps d01=4/d02=12/d03=36, identical output events, no double-fire at segment boundaries).

part2_allocator.what: For nested runs (--max-dom > 1) set XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_ALLOCATOR=platform (synchronous cudaMalloc/cudaFree -- no arena, cannot fragment, ~2x smaller resident set). JAX reads this only at GPU-backend init (first device op anywhere in the process) and importing gpuwrf already imports jax, so a runtime os.environ set is not reliably honoured; the CLI therefore RE-EXECS the same interpreter command (sys.orig_argv) ONCE with the var set in the environment (one-shot guard env to avoid a loop). Single-domain runs keep the faster default BFC arena. An explicit operator XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_ALLOCATOR is always honoured (no re-exec).
part2_allocator.tradeoff: the platform allocator is ~1.3x slower for the nested path (synchronous per-op cudaMalloc/cudaFree). Accepted: correctness/no-OOM is the gate; nested perf is a separate sprint. Single-domain path is unaffected.

peak_vram_bounded_proof.method: nvidia-smi total-used peak sampled @2Hz for the WHOLE run (platform allocator does not implement jax memory_stats); peak_total includes ~3 GiB desktop apps.
peak_vram_bounded_proof.case: the exact production failing case, via the REAL production CLI: python -m gpuwrf.cli run --max-dom 3 --hours 2 (2 output-interval segments).

before_fix_BFC_default.note: peak CREEPS toward the card limit and the m9 output transient spikes to ~full capacity -- the 24 h would fragment past 32 GiB and OOM.

after_fix_platform_allocator_reexec.note: peak FLAT and bounded at ~15.8 GiB across both segments; the ~8 GiB transient is returned to the driver immediately after each radiation/diagnostic step (resident drops to ~6 GiB). ~17 GiB headroom on the 32 GiB card.

correctness_identical.note: BFC and platform runs are identical on every correctness field; only peak VRAM (and wall) differ.

manager_24h_command: /tmp/wrf_gpu_run.sh taskset -c 0-3 env PYTHONPATH=src JAX_ENABLE_X64=true XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_PREALLOCATE=false python -m gpuwrf.cli run --input-dir /mnt/data/canairy_meteo/runs/wrf_l3/20260531_18z_l3_24h_20260601T125256Z --output-dir $HOME/.gpuwrf_nest24h_out --scratch-dir $HOME/.gpuwrf_nest24h_scratch --max-dom 3 --hours 24
manager_24h_command_note: Do NOT set XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_ALLOCATOR on the command line -- the CLI re-execs to platform automatically for --max-dom>1 (you'll see a 'nested run -- re-exec with XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_ALLOCATOR=platform' line on stderr). Output/scratch must be DISK-backed (not /tmp tmpfs); the CLI redirects /tmp scratch to $HOME/.gpuwrf_scratch automatically. Expect ~1.3x slower than BFC; the H=2 forecast was ~11 min, so 24 h is roughly ~1.5-2 h wall on this workstation.
confidence_24h_fits_32gb: HIGH. Peak is a single recurring ~8 GiB transient + a ~6 GiB working set, and the platform allocator makes peak LEAD-INDEPENDENT (no arena => no growth, no fragmentation). Measured peak 15.8 GiB at H=2 with all transients exercised; this does not grow with forecast length, leaving ~17 GiB headroom for the full 24 h.
residual_risks: Speed: the platform allocator is ~1.3x slower for nested runs (synchronous cudaMalloc/cudaFree); the per-interval compute_m9_diagnostics RRTMG recompiles dominate the output phase. Acceptable for the OOM gate; a future perf sprint could (a) cache/segment the m9 path like the single-domain _m9_snapshot, or (b) move radiation-tendency reuse into the writer to avoid the full RRTMG resolve.
The re-exec relies on sys.orig_argv (Python 3.10+, present: 3.13). Falls back to an in-process setdefault if orig_argv is unavailable.
Validated to H=2 (2 segments, all transients + both m9 output cycles exercised, peak flat). The full 24 h proof is the manager's to run with the command above.
No TOST/ensemble equivalence or CPU-speedup baseline is claimed here -- this is the OOM/memory-bounding fix only; the nesting science is the already-validated v0.11.0 path, untouched.
files_changed: src/gpuwrf/integration/nested_pipeline.py (output-interval segmented host loop + allocator setdefault)
proof_harnesses: proofs/v0120/_oom_repro.py (root-cause: where the 9.24 GiB comes from; peak flat with lead)
